title: New Testament Foundations — Mark
subtitle: The Gospel of the Urgent Servant
description: A structured study of Mark's Gospel tracing Jesus as the authoritative Servant-Messiah whose identity is revealed through powerful action, confrontation, and the way of the cross.
learning_outcomes: Explain Mark's distinctive literary style and urgency
Identify the Messianic Secret theme and its function in the narrative
Describe the structure of the second half of Mark as a passion narrative
Trace the theme of discipleship failure and restoration in Mark
Explain the significance of the centurion's confession at the cross

theological_framework.emphasis: Jesus as authoritative Servant whose power leads to the cross

units.title: Authority Revealed
units.description: Jesus' identity demonstrated through word and deed (Lessons 1–4)

units.title: Who Is Jesus?
units.description: The great confession and the way of the cross (Lessons 5–8)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What word does Mark use most frequently to show urgency?
What is the Messianic Secret and where does it first appear?
What is the turning point of Mark's Gospel in chapter 8?
What does the centurion say at the cross in Mark 15:39?
How does Mark's Gospel end in the earliest manuscripts?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — Luke
next_course.description: Study Luke's orderly account of Jesus as Savior for all people
